concerns.text: FR-002 mandates preprocessing completion 'within ≤4 hours per dataset' on a 'CPU-only runner'. This is not verifiable without defining the specific dataset size (number of participants/runs) and the exact hardware profile of the 'CPU-only runner' (e.g., vCPU count, RAM). A 4-hour limit on a 40-subject dataset is a different test than on a 4-subject dataset. The requirement must specify the dataset scope (e.g., 'for ds000246 with N=40') and the runner specs to be measurable.

FR-008 requires reporting 'consistency of primary findings across ≥6 of 9 threshold combinations'. The term 'primary findings' is not operationally defined. Does this mean the exact same cluster coordinates, the same direction of effect (sign), or the same statistical significance (p<0.05)? Without a precise definition of what constitutes a 'match' between threshold combinations, this requirement cannot be deterministically verified.

SC-005 states report framing accuracy is measured by requiring '[deferred] of group comparisons use associational language'. The use of the literal marker '[deferred]' in a Success Criterion makes it unmeasurable. A success criterion must define the condition for passing (e.g., '100% of interpretation paragraphs must avoid causal verbs'). The placeholder prevents the test from ever being executed.

The Edge Case regarding 'fewer than 10 participants' states the system 'MUST flag this... and record it... with a note that results will be framed as exploratory'. This mixes a system behavior (flagging) with a content requirement (framing). The 'framing' part is not a verifiable system output unless the spec defines the exact text string or a regex pattern that must appear in the output report. 'Framed as exploratory' is subjective without a specific template or keyword constraint.
